OTHER CONSIDERATIONS Appraisal We may order an appraisal to determine the property’s value and charge you for this appraisal. We will promptly give you a copy of any appraisal, even if you later close on this loan. Assumption If you sell or transfer the property to another person, we will allow assumption of this loan on the original terms. Homeowner's Insurance You must keep homeowner's insurance on the property, which you may obtain from a company of your choice that we find acceptable. Late Payment If your payment is more than 15 days late, we will charge a late fee of 5% of the monthly principal. Servicing If you need to service your loan, we will make your payments to us. Consent to the Use of Electronic Records and Signatures

This Consent to the Use of Electronic Records and Signatures (this "Consent") is provided by Guaranteed Rate, Inc. ("we," "us," or "our") to Marcus Bank ("you," "your," or "Customer"). This Consent applies to your accounts with us, including those accessible through the website "GuaranteedRate.com," and any other associated applications. We provide you this notice to inform you that, unless you withdraw your consent, we may communicate with you electronically and you may receive certain disclosures and forms electronically. You are entitled to receive paper copies of these documents, upon request, at no charge and you should retain this Consent for your records.

1. SCOPE OF YOUR CONSENT

By accepting this Consent, you agree that our services, affiliates, our service providers, and business partners are required by law to provide to you certain disclosures and communications in writing. However, you consent to receive these disclosures and communications electronically, including, but not limited to, communications regarding your loan application, loan account, and any transactions you undertake via the above referenced websites and applications. 

You further agree that we may satisfy any legal obligations to deliver a Communication to you in writing by delivering that Communication electronically. Any Communication we provide to you electronically will have the same legal effect as if it had been delivered in paper form.

If you do not wish to receive Communications electronically, you must withdraw your consent as described below. You are also entitled to receive paper copies of any Communication you are eligible to access electronically. 

2. ELECTRONIC SIGNATURES

You agree that your electronic signatures on any application, agreements, other documents provided to you in electronic form have the same effect as if you had signed them in writing. You also agree that your use of a key pad, mouse, or other device to select options, buttons, icons, or similar actions while using any electronic application or service we provide; or your authorization of electronic signatures; constitutes your signature — acceptance and your agreement to be bound by the terms and conditions of an electronic document.

3. HARDWARE AND SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS

To access and retain Communications, you will need: (a) a personal computer, laptop, or mobile device with an Internet access; (b) a web browser that supports TLS version 1.2 or higher; (c) a valid email address; and (d) sufficient storage space on your computer or device to save Communications or an installed printer to print them. 

It is your responsibility to ensure you have the necessary hardware and software to access Communications. We will notify you of any changes to hardware or software requirements that are necessary to access and retain your Communications. 

4. CONFIRMING YOUR ABILITY TO ACCESS

Before we provide you access to Communications, we should confirm that you are able to access information in electronic format. If you are unable to print or save the Communications, please contact us at the number below.

5. OBTAINING PAPER COPIES

You may request a paper copy of any Communication that was provided in electronic format without charge. To request a paper copy, please contact us by telephone at 1-844-327-3311 or by mail at 7845 N. Atlantic Ave, 1001, Atlanta, GA 30350.

6. KEEPING YOUR CONTACT INFORMATION CURRENT
You are responsible for ensuring that your contact information is accurate and up-to-date. You can update your contact information online or by calling us at 1-844-413-1257. You may also update your information by writing to: 7983 Albert Hart Ln, Fairfax, VA 22031. If we cannot reach you because your contact information is not current, you may miss important notices, and we may not be able to provide you with important information about your Account.

7. WITHDRAWING YOUR CONSENT
You have the right to withdraw your consent to receive Communications electronically at any time. To withdraw your consent, you must notify us by telephone at 1-844-413-1257 or in writing to 7983 Albert Hart Ln, Fairfax, VA 22031. Your withdrawal of consent will become effective after we process your request. If you withdraw your consent, we may not be able to provide you with all of the products and services you currently receive.

8. FEDERAL AND STATE LAW GOVERNING THIS CONSENT
The Consent and the relationship between you and National Government Services, Inc. will be governed by the laws of the State of Virginia, without regard to its conflict of law provisions. You agree that any dispute arising out of this Consent shall be governed by the laws of the State of Virginia and decided in the courts located therein.

9. RECORDS RETENTION AND RELIABILITY
We will retain records of your Consent and other documents signed electronically for a time that accurately reflects the information contained in such records. It is your responsibility to keep your records, as well as any hardware or software required to access or retain your records, in good condition. We cannot guarantee the retention of any communications that are not directly related to your Account history.

10. ACKNOWLEDGMENT AND AGREEMENT
By providing us with your email address or other contact information, you acknowledge and agree that you have received and read this Consent, that you understand it, and that you agree to be bound by its terms. 

This Consent will remain in effect until you provide us with a written request to withdraw it. 

Effective as of February 10, 2023, and all previous electronic communications and agreements will remain in effect unless you request otherwise. By providing your consent, you agree to receive all notices, account statements, and disclosures in full electronic form, and for the stated purposes of this Consent.

Marcus Rawb

This Notice of Assignment, Sale, or Transfer of Servicing Rights is provided to Marcus Rush in connection with loan number 7476087861 secured by the property located at 8844 Daryl Camp, East Jenksler, OK 42785, in accordance with the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act (RESPA) and its implementing regulation. The servicing of your mortgage loan, meaning the right to collect your periodic payments and to administer your loan, is or may be transferred. This Notice explains what servicing means, what the transfer does and does not change, where to direct your payments, how the sixty (60) day late- fee protection period operates, and how to submit error-resolution notices and information requests. Please read it carefully and retain it with your permanent loan records. 1. PURPOSE OF THIS NOTICE You are receiving this Notice because the servicing of your mortgage loan, that is, the right to receive and process your scheduled periodic payments of principal, interest, and any other amounts owed, is being assigned, sold, or transferred. The transfer of servicing rights is a routine practice in the mortgage industry and may occur throughout the life of your loan. This notice provides an arrangement between the prior servicer and Guaranteed Rate, Inc., as well as information regarding Real Estate Settlement Procedures and regulations. You have the right to receive further information to be informed of the new servicer and the new company that will take over the servicing of your loan. It will identify the parties involved, including your previous servicer, the new servicer, and the terms of such information. The holder of your loan may continue to be the same under the new servicing. After the transfer, the new servicer will perform the same functions for your mortgage loan, just as the prior lender or servicer did. If you have any questions regarding the transfer of servicing rights or if you need clarification of terms or change of address under this notice, please contact Marcus Rush directly to secure your loan. 2. WHAT SERVICING MEANS The term servicing refers to the administrative handling of your mortgage loan after closing. A servicer is responsible for collecting your monthly principal and interest payments, managing any escrow accounts, and, should you fail to make your mortgage payments, managing the foreclosure process and administering the loan on behalf of the holder of the mortgage. The servicer may

Elective on or about the transfer date identified in the accompanying loan documents, the servicing of your mortgage loan will be handled by Guaranteed Rate, Inc. The prior servicer will cease to accept payments on your loan as of the close of business on the day immediately preceding the transfer date, and Guaranteed Rate, Inc. will begin accepting payments on the transfer date. You should have received, or will shortly receive, a separate notice from your prior servicer confirming the date on which it will stop servicing your loan. Together, these two notices will be critical in determining your payment obligations.

If for any reason you did not receive a notice from your prior servicer, or if the dates described in the two notices do not appear to align, you should not assume that any payment obligations has lapsed. Continue making your payments to the appropriate servicer in accordance with the payment schedule provided. Contact Guaranteed Rate, Inc. at 1-815-842-1018-023256 if you have questions or would require a particular payment. Maintaining your regular payment schedule throughout the transition is essential to keeping your account current and avoiding the reporting of delinquency.

Your new servicer is Guaranteed Rate, Inc., and all payments, inquiries, and written correspondence concerning the servicing of your loan should be directed to it and after the transfer date. The business concerning your servicing can be reached at: Guaranteed Rate, Inc., 7905 Austin Ave, Elmwood Park, Illinois 60707, 025816, and has been designated as our contact for borrower assistance at 1-815-812-803261. Representatives are available to assist you with questions concerning your loan or your account. Your new servicer will also facilitate the transition of servicing from your prior servicer, Guaranteed Rate, Inc., has an active role in ensuring a seamless transition and to honor your payment instructions. It is important to note that although you may have previously authorized your prior servicer to debit your account directly, or you may have signed up for automatic payments through your prior servicer, such arrangements do not always transfer automatically between servicers.

5. WHERE TO SEND YOUR PAYMENTS
Beginning on the transfer date, you must send your mortgage payments to Guaranteed Rate, Inc. rather than to your prior servicer. Payments may be remitted to the address designated by Guaranteed Rate, Inc. for the receipt of payments, which will be sent forth in your welcome correspondence and on your periodic billing statements; general correspondence and account questions may be directed to Guaranteed Rate, Inc. at 79405 Albert Island, East Albany, DC 80150. To ensure that your payments are credited promptly and to the correct account, always include loan number 746786716 on your check, money order, or electronic remittance, and any specific remittance instructions provided by Guaranteed Rate, Inc.

Payments received by your prior servicer after the transfer date will ordinarily be forwarded to Guaranteed Rate, Inc., but you should not rely on such forwarding as a routine method of payment. To avoid delays in the crediting of your account, send all payments due on or after the transfer date directly to Guaranteed Rate, Inc. Payments you transmit through a third-party bill-payment service or your financial institution, promptly update the payer name, address, and account number on file with that service so that your payments are routed to Guaranteed Rate, Inc. for all installments coming due on and after the effective date of the transfer.

6. SIXTY-DAY LATE FEE PROTECTIONS
Federal law provides important protection to borrowers during a transfer of servicing. During the sixty (60) day period beginning on the effective date of the transfer of servicing, any payment received by your prior servicer on or before its due date, or within any applicable grace period, may not be treated as late by either servicer, and the lender may not impose any late payment charge or report it to any consumer reporting agency, solely because the payment was sent to the prior servicer rather than to Guaranteed Rate, Inc. This protection is intended to guard against penalties that could occur during the period when servicing is transferred.

To benefit from this protection, you must make each payment according to the terms of your loan documents. If you mistakenly send your payment to the wrong servicer, do not wait for that servicer to submit the payment to the correct servicer; instead, you should submit your payment directly to Guaranteed Rate, Inc. as soon as possible to avoid late charges.

After the sixty (60) day period, all payments made to Guaranteed Rate, Inc. in accordance with your standard late charges and credit-reporting practices will apply to any payment that is timely received by Guaranteed Rate, Inc.

7. YOUR CONTINUING PAYMENT OBLIGATION
The transfer of servicing does not affect, reduce, suspend, or otherwise alter your obligation to make timely payments under your loan documents. All payments made thereafter must remain fully compliant with the terms of your loan. The transfer of servicing should not affect any escrow deposits. You continue to be responsible for those payments, even if servicing is transferred to another servicer. Your payment obligations under your loan documents continue to be governed by your existing loan documents except to the extent they may be modified in a separate writing signed by the appropriate parties.

If at any time you are unable to make a scheduled payment, you should contact Guaranteed Rate, Inc. promptly at 1-815-812-4818-3226 to discuss the options that may be available to you. Do not allow uncertainty arising from the servicing transfer to cause you to withhold or deny a payment, as the protections described in this Notice are limited and do not relieve you of your underlying obligation to pay. Keeping your account current throughout and after the transition is the most reliable way to preserve your standing under the loan and to avoid the accrual of late charges or other consequences of delinquency.

1. ESCROW ACCOUNT AND INSURANCE
If your loan includes an escrow account for the payment of property taxes, hazard insurance premiums, mortgage insurance, or other charges, the administration of that account will transfer to Guaranteed Rate, Inc., along with the servicing of your loan. Guaranteed Rate, Inc. will continue to collect escrow deposits as part of your monthly payment and to disburse funds from the escrow account to pay covered items as they come due. Your escrow account balance, payment history, and any cushion permitted by applicable law will be transferred to Guaranteed Rate, Inc., and an annual escrow statement will be furnished to you in accordance with applicable requirements.

You should review your insurance policies and tax bills to confirm that Guaranteed Rate, Inc. is correctly listed, where applicable, as the party to receive dividends, notices and renewals. Guaranteed Rate, Inc. may, at its option, obtain or maintain insurance coverage: Maintaining required hazard and mortgage insurance, which is required to protect your property. In addition to any required insurance, you should maintain any insurance coverage that may be required by your documents securing your obligation under the security instrument. If required coverage lapses, Guaranteed Rate, Inc. may obtain insurance on your behalf and charge you for it. Please remember that your lender may not be as familiar with your needs as you are, and the cost of insurance purchased by your lender may be more expensive and provide less coverage than a policy you obtain on your own.

9. NOTICES OF ERROR AND INFORMATION REQUESTS
If you believe an error has occurred in the servicing of your loan, or if you need information about your loan, you have the right to submit a written notice of error or a written request for information to Guaranteed Rate, Inc. To be effective, such a notice or request should include the information described below. Your notice or request must be in writing and sent to Guaranteed Rate, Inc., at the following address: 10905 Albert Einstein Drive, East Allenhurst, DC 08150. Your written communication should include your loan number 747687681.

Upon receiving a qualifying written notice of error or information request, Guaranteed Rate, Inc. will acknowledge receipt of your notice. It will then investigate the information and respond to you in writing. Guaranteed Rate, Inc. will inform you of its determination regarding your inquiry within the timeframes established by law. You may contact the company by telephone, but any payment that is the subject of the notice, to the extent required by law. Telephone calls to customer

• Following up with the insurance adjuster as needed for resolution.

This Binding Arbitration Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into between Marcus Rush ("you" or
"Borrower") and Guaranteed Rate, Inc. ("we," "us," or "Lender") in connection with the loan

therefrom. By signing the related loan documents or otherwise accepting the terms set forth
herein, you acknowledge that you have read this Agreement, that you understand it materially
affects your legal rights, and that you agree to resolve Covered Disputes through individual
binding arbitration rather than in court before a judge or jury. Please read this Agreement

1. AGREEMENT TO ARBITRATE
You and Guaranteed Rate, Inc. agree that, except as otherwise expressly provided in this Agreement, any
Covered Dispute (as defined below) shall be resolved by neutral, binding arbitration administered on an
individual basis, and not by a court, jury, or any form of class, collective, representative, or consolidated
proceeding. This Agreement reflects the mutual intention of the parties to provide a fair, efficient, and
confidential forum for the resolution of disputes. By agreeing to arbitrate, both you and the Lender
knowingly and voluntarily relinquish certain rights that would otherwise be available in a judicial forum, as
further described in the sections that follow.
This Agreement is governed by the Federal Arbitration Act, 9 U.S.C. Section 1 et seq. (the "FAA"), and
evidences a transaction involving interstate commerce. To the extent the FAA is held inapplicable to any
issue, the arbitration laws of the OK in which the property securing the loan, located at 6844 Darryl
Camp, East Jennifer, OK 42785, is situated shall apply, provided that such laws do not conflict with the
FAA. The parties intend that this Agreement be interpreted broadly to give effect to their agreement to
arbitrate the widest range of disputes permitted by applicable law.
This Agreement applies regardless of whether the Covered Dispute arises before, during, or after the
term of the loan, and regardless of whether the loan or the underlying obligation has been paid, satisfied,
charged off, sold, assigned, or otherwise transferred. Your obligations and the Lender's obligations under
this Agreement continue notwithstanding the closing, servicing transfer, or termination of any account or
relationship between the parties.
2. DISPUTES COVERED
A "Covered Dispute" means any claim, dispute, or controversy of any kind, whether based in contract,
tort, statute, regulation, common law, equity, or otherwise, that arises out of or relates in any way to the
Retain this document with your loan file records.

loan, the loan documents, this Agreement, the property securing the loan, the origination, underwriting,
processing, closing, funding, servicing, collection, or enforcement of the loan, or any communication,
disclosure, advertisement, product, or service associated with the relationship between you and
Guaranteed Rate, Inc.. The term is to be construed in the broadest manner permitted by law.
Covered Disputes also include, without limitation, claims relating to the validity, enforceability, scope, or
interpretation of this Agreement, except as expressly reserved for judicial determination herein; claims
regarding alleged fraud, misrepresentation, breach of fiduciary duty, or unfair or deceptive acts or
practices; claims under federal or state consumer-protection, lending, privacy, or fair-credit-reporting
statutes; and claims arising from any prior loan, account, or transaction between you and the Lender.
Claims by or against the Lender's affiliates, agents, employees, officers, directors, successors, assigns,
and servicers are likewise included.
The fact that a Covered Dispute may also constitute a claim by or against a third party shall not defeat
the obligation to arbitrate as between you and Guaranteed Rate, Inc.. If any portion of a dispute falls
within the scope of this Agreement and any portion does not, the parties shall arbitrate the portion that is
covered and may stay the remainder pending the outcome of the arbitration, to the extent permitted by
applicable law and the rules of the administering body.
3. DISPUTES EXCLUDED
Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Agreement does not require arbitration of the following matters: any
action by Guaranteed Rate, Inc. to foreclose upon, or to obtain possession of, the property securing the
loan through judicial or non-judicial foreclosure, power of sale, deed in lieu, ejectment, or similar remedy
authorized under the loan documents or applicable OK law; the exercise of any self-help remedy,
including set-off; or the filing or enforcement of any lien against the collateral. The Lender's pursuit of
such remedies shall not waive its right to compel arbitration of any other Covered Dispute.
This Agreement also does not preclude either party from seeking provisional or ancillary remedies,
including temporary restraining orders, preliminary injunctions, attachment, receivership, or other interim
relief from a court of competent jurisdiction in aid of arbitration or to preserve the status quo pending
arbitration. Seeking or obtaining such relief shall not constitute a waiver of the right or obligation to
arbitrate, and any merits determination of the underlying Covered Dispute shall remain subject to
arbitration.
Nothing in this Agreement limits your right to file a complaint with, or to provide information to, any
federal, state, or local governmental agency or regulatory authority, including any banking or consumer-
protection agency, and to participate in any investigation or proceeding conducted by such authority.
This Agreement does not preclude such an authority from exercising its enforcement powers, nor does it
require you to arbitrate any claim that applicable law expressly provides may not be subjected to pre-
dispute binding arbitration.
4. SMALL-CLAIMS CARVE-OUT
Either you or Guaranteed Rate, Inc. may elect to bring and pursue an individual claim in a small-claims
court of competent jurisdiction in lieu of arbitration, provided that the claim qualifies for that court under
its jurisdictional limits and remains in that forum on an individual basis. This carve-out is intended to

preserve a simple, low-cost venue for the resolution of modest individual disputes without the
procedures associated with formal arbitration or litigation.
If a claim filed in small-claims court is transferred, removed, appealed to, or otherwise proceeds in a
court other than a small-claims court, or if the claim is amended to seek relief or to assert allegations that
exceed the jurisdiction of the small-claims court, then either party may thereafter elect to compel
arbitration of that claim under this Agreement. Any such election shall be honored by the parties, and the
dispute shall proceed in arbitration in accordance with the terms set forth herein.
A claim brought in small-claims court must be asserted only in the claimant's individual capacity and
may not be maintained as, or consolidated with, any class, collective, or representative action. The class-
action waiver and other individual-relief provisions of this Agreement apply with equal force to any matter
pursued under this small-claims carve-out.
5. ADMINISTERING BODY AND RULES
Unless the parties agree in writing to a different forum, a Covered Dispute submitted to arbitration shall
be administered by a nationally recognized, neutral arbitration administrator that maintains established
procedures for the resolution of consumer disputes, in accordance with that administrator's consumer
arbitration rules in effect at the time the arbitration is commenced. The arbitration shall be conducted by
a single neutral arbitrator who is a retired judge or an attorney experienced in consumer-finance or real-
property matters, selected in accordance with the administrator's rules.
To the extent the chosen administrator's rules conflict with this Agreement, the terms of this Agreement
shall control to the extent permitted by the administrator and applicable law. If the designated
administrator is unavailable or unwilling to administer the arbitration consistent with this Agreement, the
parties shall jointly select a comparable neutral administrator, and failing agreement, a court of
competent jurisdiction may appoint the administrator or arbitrator upon the petition of either party, as
authorized by the FAA.
The arbitrator shall have the authority to determine the arbitrability of any Covered Dispute and to resolve
any dispute concerning the interpretation, applicability, or enforceability of this Agreement, except that
the enforceability of the class-action and representative-action waiver set forth in this Agreement shall be
determined exclusively by a court and not by the arbitrator. The arbitrator shall apply the substantive law
that a court would apply and shall honor applicable statutes of limitation and claims of privilege. You may
obtain the administrator's rules and forms by contacting Guaranteed Rate, Inc. at
+1-815-812-4018x32256 or in writing at 79045 Albert Island, East Allenbury, DC 08510.
6. EFFECT OF ARBITRATION; JURY-TRIAL WAIVER
BY AGREEING TO ARBITRATE COVERED DISPUTES, YOU AND GUARANTEED RATE, INC. EACH
KNOWINGLY, VOLUNTARILY, AND INTENTIONALLY WAIVE THE RIGHT TO A TRIAL BY JURY AND THE
RIGHT TO HAVE COVERED DISPUTES DECIDED BY A JUDGE IN A COURT OF LAW. Arbitration
substitutes a private dispute-resolution process for the procedures available in court. The parties
acknowledge that this waiver is a material inducement to entering into the loan transaction and that each
has had the opportunity to consult with legal counsel of its own choosing regarding the consequences of
this waiver.
Retain this document with your loan file records.

In arbitration, the proceedings are generally more streamlined than litigation, discovery may be more
limited, and the rights to pre-trial and appellate review are substantially narrower than those available in
court. The arbitrator's decision is final and binding, subject only to the limited grounds for review
provided under the FAA. Judgment on the award rendered by the arbitrator may be entered in any court
of competent jurisdiction. The parties accept these features of arbitration in exchange for its efficiency,
finality, and reduced cost.
The arbitrator may award any relief, including monetary damages, declaratory relief, injunctive relief, and
statutory remedies, that a court could award to an individual party under applicable law, and only in favor
of the individual party seeking relief and only to the extent necessary to provide relief warranted by that
party's individual claim. The arbitrator shall issue a reasoned written award setting forth the essential
findings and conclusions on which the award is based, in accordance with the administrator's rules.
7. CLASS-ACTION AND REPRESENTATIVE WAIVER
COVERED DISPUTES SHALL BE ARBITRATED ONLY ON AN INDIVIDUAL BASIS. YOU AND
GUARANTEED RATE, INC. EACH WAIVE ANY RIGHT TO ARBITRATE OR LITIGATE ANY COVERED
DISPUTE AS A CLASS ACTION, COLLECTIVE ACTION, PRIVATE ATTORNEY-GENERAL ACTION, OR
OTHER REPRESENTATIVE PROCEEDING, OR TO SERVE AS A CLASS REPRESENTATIVE OR CLASS
MEMBER, OR TO PARTICIPATE AS A MEMBER OF A CLASS OF CLAIMANTS. The arbitrator shall have
no authority to consolidate or join the claims of more than one person and may not preside over any form
of class, collective, or representative proceeding.
The parties agree that this class-action and representative waiver is a fundamental and non-severable
element of their agreement to arbitrate. If a court or arbitrator determines that this waiver is
unenforceable or invalid as to a particular Covered Dispute or claim for relief, then, to that extent, that
particular Covered Dispute or claim shall proceed in a court of competent jurisdiction rather than in
arbitration, but all other Covered Disputes and claims shall remain subject to arbitration on an individual
basis as provided in this Agreement.
No arbitration under this Agreement shall be joined or consolidated with any other arbitration involving a
different borrower or party without the written consent of all parties to all affected arbitrations. The
prohibition on class, collective, and representative proceedings applies regardless of the forum and
survives any determination that another provision of this Agreement is unenforceable.
8. COSTS AND FEES
The party initiating an arbitration shall pay the filing fee required by the administrator, except that, upon
your written request, Guaranteed Rate, Inc. will advance or reimburse all or a portion of the filing,
administrative, and arbitrator fees attributable to a Covered Dispute that you initiate, to the extent
provided by the administrator's consumer rules or as otherwise required to ensure that the cost of

not waive any right to seek recovery of those amounts in the award.
Each party shall otherwise bear its own attorneys' fees, expert fees, and other costs incurred in
connection with the arbitration, except that the arbitrator may award attorneys' fees and costs to the
prevailing party to the extent such an award would be available under the applicable substantive law

governing the Covered Dispute, or as otherwise provided by the loan documents. The allocation of fees

recover fees and costs that you would be entitled to recover had the Covered Dispute been adjudicated

9. LOCATION AND CONDUCT OF ARBITRATION
Unless the parties agree otherwise in writing, any in-person arbitration hearing shall be held in the federal
judicial district that includes your residence or, where applicable, the OK county in which the property
located at 6844 Darryl Camp, East Jennifer, OK 42785 is situated. The arbitrator may, consistent with the

present its case. The arbitrator shall have the power to issue subpoenas, rule on the admissibility of

Except as may be required by law, to enforce or challenge the award, or to seek interim relief, the parties

10. RIGHT TO OPT OUT
You have the right to reject this Agreement and to opt out of arbitration without affecting any other term

certifications set forth herein are provided to satisfy applicable federal and state requirements and to memorialize the understandings of the parties as of February

representations made in the loan application and supporting materials. Capitalized placeholder terms are completed elsewhere within the loan package and

§ 1. PURPOSE AND SCOPE
This document consolidates several distinct borrower notices, certifications, and acknowledgments that Guaranteed Rate, Inc. is required or elects to provide in connection
with the origination of the loan referenced above. It is intended to operate together with, and not in substitution for, the promissory note, security instrument, and all other
disclosures delivered to Marcus Rush. Where any provision of this document conflicts with a more specific disclosure mandated by applicable law, the more specific
disclosure shall control to the extent of the conflict, and the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect without impairment.
Each section below addresses a separate subject matter and may be relied upon independently. The headings are provided solely for convenience and ease of reference and
do not limit, expand, or otherwise affect the substance of the acknowledgments that follow. Marcus Rush understands that signing this document does not obligate
Guaranteed Rate, Inc. to close or fund the loan and does not waive any condition that Guaranteed Rate, Inc. may impose prior to closing. Nothing herein creates any
commitment except as expressly stated in a separately executed written commitment from Guaranteed Rate, Inc..
Marcus Rush acknowledges that this document has been made available in a manner permitting review prior to execution, and that Marcus Rush has had a reasonable
opportunity to read it, to ask questions of Guaranteed Rate, Inc., and to seek independent advice from counsel or other advisors of Marcus Rush's choosing. The execution
of this document reflects Marcus Rush's informed and voluntary agreement to the matters described herein as of the date set forth above.
§ 2. RIGHT TO RECEIVE APPRAISAL
In connection with an application for a loan to be secured by a first lien on a dwelling, Guaranteed Rate, Inc. is required to provide Marcus Rush with a copy of each
written appraisal and valuation developed in connection with the application. Marcus Rush will be provided a copy promptly upon completion, or no later than the period
required by applicable law before the loan closes, whichever is earlier, even if the transaction does not close or is not consummated. This right exists regardless of whether
Marcus Rush requests a copy and is provided at no additional charge for the copy itself.
Marcus Rush may be charged for the reasonable cost of any appraisal or valuation obtained, and such cost may be disclosed separately within the closing documents and
settlement statement applicable to the loan. Guaranteed Rate, Inc. may require Marcus Rush to pay for an appraisal as a condition of processing the application. Receipt of
a copy of any appraisal does not entitle Marcus Rush to rely upon it for any purpose other than the credit transaction, and the appraisal remains the property of Guaranteed
Rate, Inc. unless otherwise required by law.
By executing this document, Marcus Rush acknowledges that the right to receive a copy of any appraisal or valuation has been disclosed and understood. Marcus Rush
may obtain additional information regarding this right by contacting Guaranteed Rate, Inc. at +1-815-812-4018x32256 or by writing to Guaranteed Rate, Inc. at 79045
Albert Island, East Allenbury, DC 08510. Marcus Rush further understands that Marcus Rush may waive the timing requirement for delivery in certain circumstances
permitted by law, provided any such waiver is made in a separate writing and does not affect Marcus Rush's underlying right to receive the copy.

Marcus Rush certifies that all statements, documents, and information provided to Guaranteed Rate, Inc. in connection with the loan application, including but not limited

Marcus Rush agrees to notify Guaranteed Rate, Inc. promptly, in writing, of any material change in the information previously furnished that occurs at any time before the

Marcus Rush acknowledges that intentionally or negligently making a false statement to Guaranteed Rate, Inc. or to a federally related mortgage lender may constitute a

§ 6. ANTI-STEERING NOTICE
Guaranteed Rate, Inc. and any mortgage loan originator acting on its behalf are prohibited by applicable law from steering Marcus Rush toward a loan in order to increase
the originator's compensation, unless the loan is in Marcus Rush's interest. Loan originator compensation may not be based on the terms of the loan other than the amount
of credit extended, and Marcus Rush is entitled to be offered loan options for which Marcus Rush likely qualifies without regard to the compensation the originator may
receive. This notice is provided in general terms and does not itself constitute an offer of any particular loan product.
Marcus Rush acknowledges that Marcus Rush has been or will be presented with information sufficient to make an informed decision among loan options for which
Marcus Rush qualifies, which may include options with the lowest available interest rate, options with the lowest total points and origination fees, and options without
certain risky features, to the extent such options are available and consistent with Marcus Rush's stated objectives. Marcus Rush understands that not all products are
available to every applicant and that eligibility depends upon underwriting and program guidelines applicable to the loan.
Any questions concerning the loan options available to Marcus Rush, the basis on which a particular product was recommended, or the compensation practices applicable
to the transaction may be directed to Guaranteed Rate, Inc. at +1-815-812-4018x32256. Marcus Rush acknowledges receipt of this anti-steering notice and affirms that
Marcus Rush has had the opportunity to inquire about alternative loan products prior to selecting the loan referenced herein.

Marcus Rush acknowledges that this affiliated business arrangement notice has been provided in general terms and that any specific affiliated relationship affecting the

+1-815-812-4018x32256.

Guaranteed Rate, Inc. will determine whether the subject property located at 6844 Darryl Camp, East Jennifer, OK 42785 is situated within an area designated as a special
flood hazard area pursuant to applicable federal standards. If the property is so located and is in a community participating in the applicable federal flood insurance

Marcus Rush understands that, if flood insurance is required, the coverage must be maintained in an amount at least equal to the outstanding principal balance of the loan

law. Should Marcus Rush fail to maintain required flood insurance, Guaranteed Rate, Inc. may, after providing any notice required by law, purchase such insurance and
charge the cost to Marcus Rush, and such force-placed coverage may be more expensive and may provide less protection than coverage Marcus Rush could obtain
independently.
Marcus Rush acknowledges that flood zone determinations may change over time and that Guaranteed Rate, Inc. may obtain updated determinations, including life-of-loan

Retain this document with your loan file records.

§ 9. HAZARD INSURANCE ACKNOWLEDGMENT
Marcus Rush agrees to obtain and maintain, at Marcus Rush's expense, hazard or property insurance covering the improvements on the subject property against loss by fire,
hazards included within the term extended coverage, and such other hazards as Guaranteed Rate, Inc. may reasonably require, in amounts and for periods that Guaranteed
Rate, Inc. requires consistent with applicable law and the terms of the security instrument. Such insurance must be carried with one or more insurers acceptable to
Guaranteed Rate, Inc. and must name Guaranteed Rate, Inc. as mortgagee or loss payee, as applicable, with appropriate provisions for notice to Guaranteed Rate, Inc. prior
to cancellation or material change.
Marcus Rush understands that the insurance must remain in effect continuously from the date of closing and throughout the term of the loan, and that Marcus Rush is
responsible for the timely payment of all premiums, whether paid directly by Marcus Rush or through an escrow account established and maintained by Guaranteed Rate,
Inc.. If Marcus Rush fails to maintain the required coverage, Guaranteed Rate, Inc. may obtain force-placed coverage protecting only Guaranteed Rate, Inc.'s interest, the
cost of which may be added to the loan balance and which may be substantially more costly and less protective than coverage Marcus Rush could obtain.
Marcus Rush acknowledges that the selection of an insurance provider is generally Marcus Rush's choice, subject to Guaranteed Rate, Inc.'s reasonable requirements
regarding the financial strength and acceptability of the insurer. Marcus Rush agrees to furnish Guaranteed Rate, Inc. with evidence of required hazard insurance prior to
closing and to deliver renewal evidence as coverage periods expire, so that there is no lapse in protection for the property securing the loan referenced as 7476087861.

This Consumer Account Terms and Conditions Agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into by
and between Marcus Rush ("you" or "Borrower") and Guaranteed Rate, Inc. ("we," "us," "our," or

1. DEFINITIONS
As used throughout this Agreement, certain capitalized terms carry the meanings assigned to them in
this section unless the context plainly requires otherwise. "Account" means the credit facility, loan, or line
of credit established between you and Guaranteed Rate, Inc. and identified by the loan number
referenced above. "Business Day" means any day other than a Saturday, Sunday, or federal banking
holiday on which our offices are open for the conduct of substantially all of our ordinary business.
"Servicer" means the entity that we designate from time to time to administer billing, collection, and
customer-service functions, which may be Guaranteed Rate, Inc. or any successor that we appoint in
accordance with applicable law.
"Indebtedness" means the aggregate of all amounts that you owe to us under this Agreement and the
related loan documents, including outstanding principal, accrued and unpaid interest, late charges,
returned-item fees, and any other sums properly assessed under the governing instruments. "Loan
Documents" means, collectively, your promissory note, this Agreement, any security instrument, and
every disclosure, rider, addendum, or amendment executed in connection with the Account. "Applicable
Law" means all federal, OK state, and local statutes, regulations, rules, and ordinances, together with all
binding judicial and administrative interpretations thereof, as the same may be amended or
supplemented from time to time during the term of the Account.
2. PROMISE TO PAY
You unconditionally promise to pay to the order of Guaranteed Rate, Inc., or to any subsequent holder of
the Loan Documents, the full amount of the Indebtedness in accordance with the payment terms
disclosed to you, together with interest accruing on the unpaid principal balance at the rate and in the
manner specified in your promissory note. Your obligation to pay is absolute and is not subject to any

condition, counterclaim, setoff, or deduction except as expressly permitted by Applicable Law. Each
payment must be made in lawful money of the United States and received by us at the address
designated for that purpose or through any electronic channel we have authorized.
If more than one person signs the Loan Documents as a borrower, each such person agrees to be jointly
and severally liable for the entire Indebtedness, meaning that we may enforce this Agreement against any
one of you, against some of you, or against all of you together, and we may do so without first
proceeding against any other obligor, guarantor, or collateral. The release or modification of the
obligations of any one borrower shall not impair the liability of any remaining borrower. You remain
obligated to perform notwithstanding any extension, indulgence, or accommodation that we may extend
to any other party at any time.
3. APPLICATION OF PAYMENTS
Unless Applicable Law requires a different order, we will apply each payment we receive and accept first
to any accrued and unpaid interest, then to outstanding principal, and thereafter to escrow obligations,
late charges, and other fees lawfully assessed against the Account, in the sequence we determine in our
reasonable discretion. Payments received after the daily or monthly cutoff that we establish may be
credited as of the next Business Day. We are not obligated to accept any partial payment, and our
acceptance of a partial or late payment shall not constitute a waiver of our right to require payment in full
or to declare a default for any amount that remains unpaid.
If you direct that a payment be applied in a particular manner, or if you tender a payment marked "paid in
full" or accompanied by any restrictive endorsement or condition, we may accept and process that
payment without being bound by the stated restriction, and no such notation shall operate as an accord
and satisfaction unless we expressly agree in a separate writing signed by an authorized officer. Funds
tendered toward future installments do not relieve you of the obligation to make each scheduled payment
as it becomes due, nor do they change the due date or the amount of any subsequent installment.
4. LATE CHARGES AND FEES
If any scheduled payment is not received in full by the end of the grace period disclosed in your Loan
Documents, you agree to pay a late charge in the amount and at the rate permitted by Applicable Law
and set forth in those documents. A late charge is assessed to compensate us for the administrative cost
and inconvenience of handling a delinquent payment and does not constitute interest, a penalty, or our
consent to late payment. We may assess such a charge for each delinquent installment, and the
imposition of one or more late charges shall not limit any other right or remedy otherwise available to us
under this Agreement.
You further agree to reimburse us for any fee that we incur or that is permitted under Applicable Law in
connection with a check, draft, or electronic payment that is returned or dishonored for any reason, as
well as for charges associated with property inspections, payoff statements, and similar services lawfully
provided in the servicing of your Account. All fees properly assessed and remaining unpaid become part
of the Indebtedness and may accrue interest at the rate applicable to principal. We will disclose the
nature and amount of any such fee in the manner required by Applicable Law before or at the time it is
charged to the Account.
Retain this document with your loan file records.

5. DEFAULT
You will be in default under this Agreement upon the occurrence of any of the following events: your
failure to make any payment when due; your failure to perform any other material obligation set forth in
the Loan Documents; the discovery that any representation or warranty you made was materially false or
misleading when made; your insolvency, assignment for the benefit of creditors, or the commencement
of any voluntary or involuntary bankruptcy proceeding affecting you; or the levy, seizure, or attachment of
any collateral securing the Account. Default also occurs if any other event identified as an event of
default in your promissory note or security instrument takes place.
Except where Applicable Law provides otherwise or where the nature of the default makes notice or cure
impracticable, we will give you any notice of default and any right to cure that Applicable Law requires
before we accelerate the Indebtedness or pursue remedies. A notice of default will, to the extent
required, specify the nature of the default, the action necessary to cure it, a date by which the cure must
be completed, and the consequences of failing to cure within the stated period. Our failure to declare a
default upon the occurrence of one event shall not constitute a waiver of our right to declare a default
upon any subsequent or continuing event.
6. REMEDIES UPON DEFAULT
Upon the occurrence of a default that remains uncured beyond any applicable cure period, we may, at
our option and to the extent permitted by Applicable Law, declare the entire unpaid Indebtedness,
together with all accrued interest and lawfully assessed charges, immediately due and payable without
further demand or notice, all of which you hereby waive to the fullest extent the law allows. We may
thereafter exercise any and all rights and remedies available to us under the Loan Documents, at law, or
in equity, including the right to enforce any security interest, to foreclose upon collateral, and to pursue
collection of any deficiency that remains after the application of proceeds.
All of our rights and remedies are cumulative and not exclusive, and we may exercise them concurrently,
successively, or in any order we choose. No delay or omission on our part in exercising any right or
remedy shall impair that right or remedy or be construed as a waiver thereof. You agree to pay, to the
extent permitted by Applicable Law, the reasonable costs and expenses we incur in enforcing this
Agreement, including court costs and reasonable attorneys' fees, whether incurred before, during, or
after any judicial or bankruptcy proceeding, and such amounts shall be added to the Indebtedness.
7. CHANGES TO TERMS
We may amend, modify, or supplement the terms of this Agreement from time to time to the extent
permitted by Applicable Law and by your Loan Documents. Where a change requires your advance
notice or consent, we will provide notice in the form, manner, and within the timeframe that Applicable
Law prescribes, which may include written notice mailed to the most recent address reflected in our
records or, where you have consented, an electronic communication. Certain terms, including a fixed
contractual interest rate, may not be altered except as expressly authorized by the Loan Documents or
as you and we may agree in a signed writing.
If a change in law or regulation requires us to modify any provision of this Agreement, that provision shall
be deemed amended to the minimum extent necessary to bring it into compliance, and the remainder of

and nothing in this section shall be read to diminish any protection afforded to you by mandatory

You agree that any action or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement may be brought in the
state or federal courts located in or serving the jurisdiction in which the Account is administered or the

defend a proceeding in another permissible venue, nor does it waive any protection that cannot be

9. SEVERABILITY
If any provision of this Agreement, or the application of any provision to any person or circumstance, is
held by a court or other tribunal of competent jurisdiction to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable, that
determination shall not affect the validity or enforceability of any other provision, and the remaining
provisions shall continue in full force and effect as though the offending provision had never been
included. The parties intend that each provision of this Agreement be severable, and that the Agreement
be enforced to the maximum extent permitted by Applicable Law notwithstanding the partial invalidity of
any term.
Where a provision is found to be unenforceable only because it exceeds a limit imposed by Applicable
Law, that provision shall be deemed reduced or reformed to the maximum extent permitted by law and
enforced as so modified, in order to give effect, to the greatest degree possible, to the original intent of
the parties. In particular, if any charge collected or contracted for under this Agreement is determined to
exceed a lawful maximum, the excess shall be applied to reduce principal or, if there is no principal
outstanding, refunded to you in the manner required by Applicable Law.
10. ASSIGNMENT

Account, or any of our rights and obligations under them, in whole or in part and at any time, to any
person or entity without notice to you except as Applicable Law may require, and without your consent.

servicing of the Account to a Servicer at any time, and you agree to remit payments and direct inquiries
as the current Servicer directs.
You may not assign, delegate, or transfer your rights or obligations under this Agreement to any other
person without our prior written consent, and any purported assignment by you made without such
consent shall be void and of no effect. The sale or transfer of any property securing the Account does
not relieve you of liability for the Indebtedness unless we expressly agree in writing to release you. This
Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of you and us and our respective heirs,
executors, administrators, successors, and permitted assigns.
11. NOTICES
Except where Applicable Law specifies a different method, any notice that you are required or permitted
to give us under this Agreement must be in writing and delivered to Guaranteed Rate, Inc. at 79045
Albert Island, East Allenbury, DC 08510, or to such other address as we may designate in a subsequent
notice, and shall be deemed given when actually received by us. Any notice that we are required or
permitted to give you may be delivered by first-class mail, postage prepaid, addressed to you at 6844
Darryl Camp, East Jennifer, OK 42785 or at the most recent address shown in our records, and shall be
deemed given on the date of mailing unless Applicable Law requires actual receipt.
If more than one person is obligated under the Account, notice given to any one of you shall constitute
notice to all of you for purposes of this Agreement. You agree to notify us promptly in writing of any
change in your mailing address, telephone number, or other contact information, and you remain
responsible for ensuring that we have current information through which to reach you. Where you have
consented to receive communications electronically, we may provide notices and disclosures by
electronic means to the extent permitted by Applicable Law, and such delivery shall have the same effect
as delivery by mail. You may reach us by telephone at +1-815-812-4018x32256.
12. NO WAIVER
No failure or delay on our part in exercising any right, power, or privilege under this Agreement, and no
course of dealing between you and us, shall operate as a waiver of that right, power, or privilege, nor
shall any single or partial exercise of any right preclude any further exercise of that right or the exercise of
any other right. Any waiver of any provision of this Agreement, or of any default, must be in writing and
signed by an authorized officer of Guaranteed Rate, Inc. to be effective, and any such waiver shall be
limited to the specific provision or default expressly identified.
Our acceptance of one or more late, partial, or irregular payments, our extension of time for performance,
our forbearance from exercising any remedy, or our agreement to any single accommodation shall not
constitute a waiver of our right to insist upon strict compliance with the terms of this Agreement
thereafter, nor shall it establish a custom or course of dealing that varies those terms. To the extent
permitted by Applicable Law, you waive presentment, demand for payment, notice of dishonor, protest,
and notice of protest with respect to the Indebtedness.
Retain this document with your loan file records.

13. ENTIRE AGREEMENT
This Agreement, together with your promissory note, security instrument, and the other Loan Documents,
constitutes the entire agreement between you and Guaranteed Rate, Inc. with respect to the Account
and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous understandings, representations, negotiations, and
agreements, whether oral or written, relating to its subject matter. You acknowledge that you have not
relied upon any statement, promise, or representation that is not expressly set forth in the Loan
Documents, and that no oral statement by any of our representatives shall be deemed to modify or
supplement the written terms.
In the event of any direct conflict between the provisions of this Agreement and those of another Loan
Document, the documents shall be read together and harmonized to the greatest extent possible; where
harmonization is not possible, the provision affording you the greater protection under Applicable Law, or
the provision specifically governing the matter in question, shall control. The headings used in this
Agreement are inserted for convenience of reference only and shall not affect the interpretation of any
provision. This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original
and all of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument, effective as of February 10, 2024.

Marcus Rush

Privacy Notice NOTICE & WELCOME This Privacy Notice describes how Guaranteed Rate, Inc. (“Guaranteed Rate,” “we,” “us,” or “our”) collects, shares, and protects the personal information of consumers who apply for or obtain identified financial products or services through Guaranteed Rate. This notice is required by law and usually provides you with details about this privacy policy. We may occasionally amend this notice, and we encourage you to review it periodically. 1. WHY WE PROVIDE THIS NOTICE Financial companies choose how they share personal information, and federal law gives consumers the right to limit some but not all sharing. Federal law also requires us to tell you how we collect, share, and protect your personal information. Please read this notice carefully to understand what we do. 2. INFORMATION WE COLLECT The types of personal information we collect depends on the product or service. The information we collect applies to the product or service you obtained from us. This information may include: – Name, address, social security number, and financial information. 3. HOW WE SHARE YOUR INFORMATION All financial companies need to share customers' personal information to run their everyday business. In the section below, we describe the reasons that we share your personal information. 4. REASONS YOU CANNOT LIMIT SHARING There are some criteria entirely different from what is presented here that might exempt you from limiting the sharing of your personal information. 5. SHARING WITH AFFILIATES Affiliates are companies we control or that are under common control with us. We may share with our affiliates the information necessary to serve you better and with greater efficiency, but you will receive additional documents that specify applicable laws.

We may share information about you with affiliates (other institutions that provide information about you, including information on your application and how you are repaying loans) and nonaffiliates (third parties that are not your affiliates) for our marketing and commercial purposes. This includes sharing information about your loans and deposits, your payment history, and other information that we obtain from you, your application, or your account.

5. SHARING WITH NONAFFILIATES  
Nonaffiliates are companies not related by common ownership or control, and we may share nonpublic personal information about you with nonaffiliated third parties as permitted by law. We may share personal information with third parties such as service providers, marketing companies, and credit bureaus, in order to fulfill transactions you request, provide products and services you request, and protect our interests. Such disclosures may include sharing information with others for marketing and promotional purposes, and with service providers who assist us in our business operations.

6. JOINT MARKETING ARRANGEMENTS  
As a member, you may participate in joint marketing arrangements between nonaffiliated financial companies that together market financial products or services to you. Generally, you may know of such arrangements when we, or another financial institution, tell you about a joint offer or provide information to you about financial products and services offered to you by another party.

7. YOUR CHOICES AND HOW TO LIMIT SHARING  
If you are a current member, we may share information about you through the practices described in this notice. When you are no longer a member, we continue to adhere to our privacy policies and practices and will limit sharing with nonaffiliated third parties in accordance with applicable law; however, we will share with nonaffiliates to the extent permitted by law as outlined in this notice.

8. HOW WE PROTECT YOUR INFORMATION  
To protect your personal information, we maintain physical, electronic, and procedural safeguards that comply with applicable laws. We restrict access to your personal information to those employees and agents who need to know that information to provide products or services to you.

9. INFORMATION FOR FORMER CUSTOMERS  
If you become a former member, our policies and practices concerning the sharing of your nonpublic personal information will continue to apply to you and such information will not be shared, except as necessary to service your accounts or as required or permitted by law.

10. CHANGES TO THIS NOTICE AND HOW TO CONTACT US  
We reserve the right to amend the Privacy Notice at any time. If we make material changes to our information practices, we will provide you a notice of these changes, and we will notify you of our practices regarding information disclosure as required by law. If you have any questions, please feel free to contact us at the number provided on the back of this notice.

GUARANTEED RATE, INC. - GENERAL DISCLOSURES AND BORROWER
ACKNOWLEDGMENTS
General Disclosures and Acknowledgments
Issued to Marcus rush

LENDER Guaranteed Rate, Inc.
LOAN NO. 7476087861
DATE 02/10/2024
BORROWER Marcus Rush

This consolidated General Disclosures and Borrower Acknowledgments document is furnished by Guaranteed Rate, Inc. to Marcus Rush in connection with the
loan identified as 7476087861 and secured by the real property located at 6844 Darryl Camp, East Jennifer, OK 42785 in the State of OK. The disclosures and
certifications set forth herein are provided to satisfy applicable federal and state requirements and to memorialize the understandings of the parties as of February
10, 2024. By reviewing and executing this document, Marcus Rush acknowledges receipt of the notices described below and affirms the accuracy of the
representations made in the loan application and supporting materials. Capitalized placeholder terms are completed elsewhere within the loan package and
incorporated herein by reference for all purposes.
§ 1. PURPOSE AND SCOPE
This document consolidates several distinct borrower notices, certifications, and acknowledgments that Guaranteed Rate, Inc. is required or elects to provide in connection
with the origination of the loan referenced above. It is intended to operate together with, and not in substitution for, the promissory note, security instrument, and all other
disclosures delivered to Marcus Rush. Where any provision of this document conflicts with a more specific disclosure mandated by applicable law, the more specific
disclosure shall control to the extent of the conflict, and the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect without impairment.
Each section below addresses a separate subject matter and may be relied upon independently. The headings are provided solely for convenience and ease of reference and
do not limit, expand, or otherwise affect the substance of the acknowledgments that follow. Marcus Rush understands that signing this document does not obligate
Guaranteed Rate, Inc. to close or fund the loan and does not waive any condition that Guaranteed Rate, Inc. may impose prior to closing. Nothing herein creates any
commitment except as expressly stated in a separately executed written commitment from Guaranteed Rate, Inc..
Marcus Rush acknowledges that this document has been made available in a manner permitting review prior to execution, and that Marcus Rush has had a reasonable
opportunity to read it, to ask questions of Guaranteed Rate, Inc., and to seek independent advice from counsel or other advisors of Marcus Rush's choosing. The execution
of this document reflects Marcus Rush's informed and voluntary agreement to the matters described herein as of the date set forth above.
§ 2. RIGHT TO RECEIVE APPRAISAL
In connection with an application for a loan to be secured by a first lien on a dwelling, Guaranteed Rate, Inc. is required to provide Marcus Rush with a copy of each
written appraisal and valuation developed in connection with the application. Marcus Rush will be provided a copy promptly upon completion, or no later than the period
required by applicable law before the loan closes, whichever is earlier, even if the transaction does not close or is not consummated. This right exists regardless of whether
Marcus Rush requests a copy and is provided at no additional charge for the copy itself.
Marcus Rush may be charged for the reasonable cost of any appraisal or valuation obtained, and such cost may be disclosed separately within the closing documents and
settlement statement applicable to the loan. Guaranteed Rate, Inc. may require Marcus Rush to pay for an appraisal as a condition of processing the application. Receipt of
a copy of any appraisal does not entitle Marcus Rush to rely upon it for any purpose other than the credit transaction, and the appraisal remains the property of Guaranteed
Rate, Inc. unless otherwise required by law.
By executing this document, Marcus Rush acknowledges that the right to receive a copy of any appraisal or valuation has been disclosed and understood. Marcus Rush
may obtain additional information regarding this right by contacting Guaranteed Rate, Inc. at +1-815-812-4018x32256 or by writing to Guaranteed Rate, Inc. at 79045
Albert Island, East Allenbury, DC 08510. Marcus Rush further understands that Marcus Rush may waive the timing requirement for delivery in certain circumstances
permitted by law, provided any such waiver is made in a separate writing and does not affect Marcus Rush's underlying right to receive the copy.
§ 3. ACCURACY OF INFORMATION
Marcus Rush certifies that all statements, documents, and information provided to Guaranteed Rate, Inc. in connection with the loan application, including but not limited
to information regarding income, employment, assets, liabilities, credit history, and the subject property, are true, complete, and accurate as of the date provided. Marcus
Rush understands that Guaranteed Rate, Inc. is relying upon the accuracy of this information in evaluating the application, in making its credit decision, and in determining
the terms upon which the loan may be offered. Any material misstatement or omission may constitute grounds for denial or for declaring the loan in default after closing.
Marcus Rush agrees to notify Guaranteed Rate, Inc. promptly, in writing, of any material change in the information previously furnished that occurs at any time before the
loan is closed and funded. Such changes include, without limitation, a change in employment status, a material reduction in income, the incurrence of new debt, or a
change in the intended use or occupancy of the subject property. Marcus Rush acknowledges that Guaranteed Rate, Inc. may verify any information provided through third
parties, consumer reporting agencies, employers, financial institutions, and other appropriate sources at any time.
Marcus Rush acknowledges that intentionally or negligently making a false statement to Guaranteed Rate, Inc. or to a federally related mortgage lender may constitute a
violation of applicable federal and state law and may subject Marcus Rush to civil and criminal penalties, including fines and imprisonment. Marcus Rush affirms that
Marcus Rush has reviewed the information submitted and that it accurately reflects Marcus Rush's financial condition and the facts surrounding the requested transaction
as of February 10, 2024.
§ 4. OCCUPANCY CERTIFICATION
Marcus Rush certifies the intended occupancy status of the subject property located at 6844 Darryl Camp, East Jennifer, OK 42785 as represented in the loan application. If
the property has been represented as Marcus Rush's principal residence, Marcus Rush certifies an intention to occupy, establish, and use the property as Marcus Rush's
principal residence within the period customarily required following closing, and to continue to occupy the property as a principal residence for the period required by the
loan documents, unless otherwise agreed by Guaranteed Rate, Inc. in writing or required by extenuating circumstances beyond Marcus Rush's reasonable control.
Marcus Rush understands that the occupancy representation is a material factor in Guaranteed Rate, Inc.'s underwriting decision and in the pricing and terms of the loan,
and that loans secured by a principal residence may carry different terms than loans secured by second homes or investment properties. Marcus Rush agrees that any
